BATT2_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT2_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT2_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT2_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT2_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT2_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT2_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT2_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT2_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT2_ESC_INDEX.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT2_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT2_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT2_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT2_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT2_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT2_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT2_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT2_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT2_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT2_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT2_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT2_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT2_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.1: MPPT reports input voltage and current
bitmask.6: Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
bitmask.7: Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
bitmask.8: Battery is for internal autopilot use only
bitmask.9: Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
bitmask.10: Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT2_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT2_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT2_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT2_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT2_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT2_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT2_VOLT_PIN.display_name: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATT2_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT2_WATT_MAX.description: If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

BATT3_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT3_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT3_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT3_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT3_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT3_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT3_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT3_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT3_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT3_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT3_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT3_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT3_ESC_INDEX.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT3_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT3_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT3_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT3_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT3_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT3_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT3_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT3_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT3_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT3_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT3_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT3_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT3_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT3_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT3_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT3_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.1: MPPT reports input voltage and current
bitmask.6: Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
bitmask.7: Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
bitmask.8: Battery is for internal autopilot use only
bitmask.9: Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
bitmask.10: Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT3_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT3_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT3_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT3_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT3_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT3_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT3_VOLT_PIN.display_name: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATT3_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT3_WATT_MAX.description: If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

BATT4_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT4_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT4_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT4_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT4_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT4_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT4_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT4_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT4_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT4_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT4_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT4_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT4_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT4_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT4_ESC_INDEX.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT4_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT4_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT4_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT4_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT4_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT4_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT4_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT4_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT4_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT4_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT8_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT8_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT8_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT8_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT8_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT8_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT8_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT8_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT8_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT8_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT8_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT8_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT8_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT8_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.1: MPPT reports input voltage and current
bitmask.6: Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
bitmask.7: Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
bitmask.8: Battery is for internal autopilot use only
bitmask.9: Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
bitmask.10: Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT8_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT8_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT8_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT8_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT8_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT8_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT8_VOLT_PIN.display_name: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATT8_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT8_WATT_MAX.description: If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

BATT9_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATT9_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATT9_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT9_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT9_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT9_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT9_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT9_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT9_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT9_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT9_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT9_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT9_ESC_INDEX.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT9_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTD_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTD_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTD_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTD_ESC_INDEX.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATTD_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTD_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTD_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTD_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTD_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATTD_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTD_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTD_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTD_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTD_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTD_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATTD_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTD_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATTD_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.1: MPPT reports input voltage and current
bitmask.6: Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
bitmask.7: Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
bitmask.8: Battery is for internal autopilot use only
bitmask.9: Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
bitmask.10: Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATTD_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTD_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTD_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTD_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATTD_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTD_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATTD_VOLT_PIN.display_name: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATTD_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTD_WATT_MAX.description: If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

BATTE_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATTE_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATTE_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTE_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATTE_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTE_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BRD_ALT_CONFIG.description: Select an alternative hardware configuration. A value of zero selects the default configuration for this board. Other values are board specific. Please see the documentation for your board for details on any alternative configuration values that may be available.

BRD_BOOT_DELAY.description: This adds a delay in milliseconds to boot to ensure peripherals initialise fully

BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN.description: Arming check will fail if temp is lower than this margin below BRD_HEAT_TARG. 0 disables the low temperature check

BRD_HEAT_TARG.description: Board heater target temperature for boards with controllable heating units. Set to -1 to disable the heater, please reboot after setting to -1.

BRD_IDLE_STATS.display_name: Capture and calculate true CPU load using idle threads
BRD_IDLE_STATS.description: Capture and calculate true CPU load using idle threads

BRD_IO_DSHOT.description: This loads the DShot firmware on the IO co-processor

BRD_IO_ENABLE.description: This allows for the IO co-processor on boards with an IOMCU to be disabled. Setting to 2 will enable the IOMCU but not attempt to update firmware on startup

bitmask.5: Write protect firmware flash on reboot
bitmask.6: Write protect bootloader flash on reboot
bitmask.8: Disable board arming gpio output change on arm/disarm

BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL.description: This sets the voltage max for PWM output pulses. 0 for 3.3V and 1 for 5V output. On boards with an IOMCU that support this parameter this option only affects the 8 main outputs, not the 6 auxiliary outputs. Using 5V output can help to reduce the impact of ESC noise interference corrupting signals to the ESCs.

BRD_SAFETYOPTION.description: This controls the activation of the safety button. It allows you to control if the safety button can be used for safety enable and/or disable, and whether the button is only active when disarmed

bitmask.3: Force safety on when the aircraft disarms

BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT.display_name: Sets default state of the safety switch
BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT.description: This controls the default state of the safety switch at startup. When set to 1 the safety switch will start in the safe state (flashing) at boot. When set to zero the safety switch will start in the unsafe state (solid) at startup. Note that if a safety switch is fitted the user can still control the safety state after startup using the switch. The safety state can also be controlled in software using a MAVLink message.

BRD_SAFETY_MASK.display_name: Outputs which ignore the safety switch state
BRD_SAFETY_MASK.description: A bitmask which controls what outputs can move while the safety switch has not been pressed

BRD_SBUS_OUT.description: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz

BRD_SD_FENCE.description: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in fence.stg for fence storage.

BRD_SD_MISSION.description: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in mission.stg for waypoint storage. Each waypoint uses 15 bytes.

BRD_SD_SLOWDOWN.description: This is a scaling factor to slow down microSD operation. It can be used on flight board and microSD card combinations where full speed is not reliable. For normal full speed operation a value of 0 should be used.

BRD_SER1_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 1 (telemetry 1). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup. Note that the PX4v1 does not have hardware flow control pins on this port, so you should leave this disabled.

BRD_SER2_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 2 (telemetry 2). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER3_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 3. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER4_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 4. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER5_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 5. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER6_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 6. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER7_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 7. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER8_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 8. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SERIAL_NUM.description: User-defined serial number of this vehicle, it can be any arbitrary number you want and has no effect on the autopilot

BRD_TYPE.description: This allows selection of a PX4 or VRBRAIN board type. If set to zero then the board type is auto-detected (PX4)

BRD_VBUS_MIN.description: Minimum voltage on the autopilot power rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BRD_VSERVO_MIN.description: Minimum voltage on the servo rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BRD_RADIO_ABLVL.description: This sets the minimum RSSI of an auto-bind packet for it to be accepted. This should be set so that auto-bind will only happen at short range to minimise the change of an auto-bind happening accidentially

BRD_RADIO_ABTIME.description: When non-zero this sets the time with no transmitter packets before we start looking for auto-bind packets.

BRD_RADIO_BZOFS.description: Set transmitter buzzer note adjustment (adjust frequency up)

BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.display_name: Put radio into FCC test mode
BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.description: If this is enabled then the radio will continuously transmit as required for FCC testing. The transmit channel is set by the value of the parameter. The radio will not work for RC input while this is enabled

BRD_RADIO_PPSCH.description: Channel to show received packet-per-second rate, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_SIGCH.description: Channel to show receive RSSI signal strength, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_STKMD.description: This selects between different stick input modes. The default is mode2, which has throttle on the left stick and pitch on the right stick. You can instead set mode1, which has throttle on the right stick and pitch on the left stick.

BRD_RADIO_TELEM.description: If this is non-zero then telemetry packets will be sent over DSM

BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.display_name: Set radio to factory test channel
BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.description: This sets the radio to a fixed test channel for factory testing. Using a fixed channel avoids the need for binding in factory testing.

BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH.description: Channel to show telemetry packets-per-second value, as received at TX

BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.display_name: RSSI value channel for telemetry data on transmitter
BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.description: Channel to show telemetry RSSI value as received by TX

BRD_RADIO_TXMAX.description: Set transmitter maximum transmit power (from 1 to 8)

BRD_RADIO_TXPOW.description: Set telemetry transmit power. This is the power level (from 1 to 8) for telemetry packets sent from the RX to the TX

BRD_RADIO_TYPE.display_name: Set type of direct attached radio
BRD_RADIO_TYPE.description: This enables support for direct attached radio receivers

BRD_RTC_TYPES.description: Specifies which sources of UTC time will be accepted

BRD_RTC_TZ_MIN.description: Adds offset in +- minutes from UTC to calculate local time

BTN_ENABLE.description: This enables the button checking module. When this is disabled the parameters for setting button inputs are not visible

BTN_FUNC1.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

values.154: ArmDisarm with Quadplane AirMode (4.2 and higher)
values.155: Set roll pitch and yaw trim to current servo and RC
values.180: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC2.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

values.154: ArmDisarm with Quadplane AirMode (4.2 and higher)
values.155: Set roll pitch and yaw trim to current servo and RC
values.180: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC3.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

values.154: ArmDisarm with Quadplane AirMode (4.2 and higher)
values.155: Set roll pitch and yaw trim to current servo and RC
values.180: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC4.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

values.154: ArmDisarm with Quadplane AirMode (4.2 and higher)
values.155: Set roll pitch and yaw trim to current servo and RC
values.180: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_OPTIONS1.description: Options for Pin 1. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS2.description: Options for Pin 2. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS3.description: Options for Pin 3. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS4.description: Options for Pin 4. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_PIN1.description: Digital pin number for first button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN2.description: Digital pin number for second button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN3.description: Digital pin number for third button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN4.description: Digital pin number for fourth button input. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_REPORT_SEND.description: The duration in seconds that a BUTTON_CHANGE report is repeatedly sent to the GCS regarding a button changing state. Note that the BUTTON_CHANGE message is MAVLink2 only.

CAM_AUTO_ONLY.description: When enabled, trigging by distance is done in AUTO mode only.

CAM_MAX_ROLL.description: Postpone shooting if roll is greater than limit. (0=Disable, will shoot regardless of roll).

CAM1_DURATION.description: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM1_FEEDBAK_PIN.description: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM1_FEEDBAK_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

CAM1_HFOV.description: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN.display_name: Camera minimum time interval between photos
CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN.description: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM1_MNT_INST.description: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

bitmask.0: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM1_RELAY_ON.description: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM1_SERVO_OFF.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM1_SERVO_ON.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM1_TRIGG_DIST.description: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM1_TYPE.description: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM1_VFOV.description: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM1_RC_BTN_DELY.display_name: RunCam button delay before allowing further button presses
CAM1_RC_BTN_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_BT_DELAY.display_name: RunCam boot delay before allowing updates
CAM1_RC_BT_DELAY.description: Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_CONTROL.description: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAM1_RC_FEATURES.description: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM1_RC_MDE_DELY.display_name: RunCam mode delay before allowing further button presses
CAM1_RC_MDE_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_TYPE.description: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.

CAM2_DURATION.description: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM2_FEEDBAK_PIN.description: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM2_FEEDBAK_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

CAM2_HFOV.description: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN.display_name: Camera minimum time interval between photos
CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN.description: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM2_MNT_INST.description: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

bitmask.0: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM2_RELAY_ON.description: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM2_SERVO_OFF.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM2_SERVO_ON.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM2_TRIGG_DIST.description: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM2_TYPE.description: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM2_VFOV.description: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_RC_BTN_DELY.display_name: RunCam button delay before allowing further button presses
CAM2_RC_BTN_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_BT_DELAY.display_name: RunCam boot delay before allowing updates
CAM2_RC_BT_DELAY.description: Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_CONTROL.description: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAM2_RC_FEATURES.description: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM2_RC_MDE_DELY.display_name: RunCam mode delay before allowing further button presses
CAM2_RC_MDE_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_TYPE.description: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.

CAN_LOGLEVEL.description: Loglevel for recording initialisation and debug information from CAN Interface

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.display_name: Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driver
CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.description: Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2.display_name: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing
CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2.description: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_ID.description: Node ID to send ECU throttle messages to. Set to zero to disable ECU throttle messages. Set to 255 to broadcast to all ECUs.

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_RT.description: Output rate of ECU command messages

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_BM.description: Bitmask defining which ESC (motor) channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_RT.description: Output rate of ESC command messages

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask defining which servo channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_RT.description: Output rate of servo command messages

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_BM.display_name: Output channels to be transmitted as ESC over DroneCAN
CAN_D1_UC_ESC_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a ESC command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_OF.description: Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo outputs 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV.display_name: Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.
CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV.description: Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

CAN_D1_UC_NODE.description: DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network

CAN_D1_UC_NTF_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

CAN_D1_UC_POOL.description: Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads

CAN_D1_UC_RLY_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

CAN_D1_UC_S1_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S1_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S1_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S1_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D1_UC_S1_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S2_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S2_IDX.display_name: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D1_UC_S2_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S2_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S2_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D1_UC_S2_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S3_BD.display_name: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node
CAN_D1_UC_S3_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S3_IDX.display_name: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D1_UC_S3_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S3_NOD.description: CAN node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S3_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D1_UC_S3_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_BM.display_name: Output channels to be transmitted as servo over DroneCAN
CAN_D1_UC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a servo command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

CAN_D2_PROTOCOL.display_name: Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driver
CAN_D2_PROTOCOL.description: Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

CAN_D2_PROTOCOL2.display_name: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing
CAN_D2_PROTOCOL2.description: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

CAN_D3_UC_RLY_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

CAN_D3_UC_S1_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S1_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S1_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S1_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S1_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S2_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S2_IDX.display_name: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D3_UC_S2_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S2_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S2_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S2_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S3_IDX.display_name: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D3_UC_S3_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S3_NOD.description: CAN node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S3_PRO.display_name: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S3_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_SRV_BM.display_name: Output channels to be transmitted as servo over DroneCAN
CAN_D3_UC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a servo command over DroneCAN

CAN_D3_UC_SRV_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

CAN_P1_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P1_DRIVER.display_name: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P1_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_P2_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P2_DRIVER.display_name: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P2_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P2_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_P3_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P3_DRIVER.display_name: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P3_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P3_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.description: CAN Interface ID to be routed to SLCAN, 0 means no routing

CAN_SLCAN_SDELAY.description: Duration after which slcan starts after setting SERNUM in seconds.

CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.description: Serial Port ID to be used for temporary SLCAN iface, -1 means no temporary serial. This parameter is automatically reset on reboot or on timeout. See CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT for timeout details

CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.description: Duration of inactivity after which SLCAN is switched back to original driver in seconds.

CHUTE_ALT_MIN.display_name: Parachute min altitude in meters above home
CHUTE_ALT_MIN.description: Parachute min altitude above home.  Parachute will not be released below this altitude.  0 to disable alt check.

CHUTE_CRT_SINK.display_name: Critical sink speed rate in m/s to trigger emergency parachute
CHUTE_CRT_SINK.description: Release parachute when critical sink rate is reached

CHUTE_DELAY_MS.description: Delay in millseconds between motor stop and chute release

CHUTE_SERVO_OFF.description: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is not released

CHUTE_SERVO_ON.description: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is released

CHUTE_TYPE.display_name: Parachute release mechanism type (relay or servo)
CHUTE_TYPE.description: Parachute release mechanism type (relay number in versions prior to 4.5, or servo). Values 0-3 all are relay. Relay number used for release is set by RELAYx_FUNCTION in 4.5 or later.

COMPASS_AUTODEC.description: Enable or disable the automatic calculation of the declination based on gps location

COMPASS_AUTO_ROT.description: When enabled this will automatically check the orientation of compasses on successful completion of compass calibration. If set to 2 then external compasses will have their orientation automatically corrected.

values.3: use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

COMPASS_CAL_FIT.description: This controls the fitness level required for a successful compass calibration. A lower value makes for a stricter fit (less likely to pass). This is the value used for the primary magnetometer. Other magnetometers get double the value.

COMPASS_CUS_PIT.description: Compass mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_ROLL.description: Compass mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_YAW.description: Compass mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_DEC.description: An angle to compensate between the true north and magnetic north

COMPASS_DEV_ID.description: Compass device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID2.description: Second compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID3.description: Third compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID4.description: Extra 4th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID5.description: Extra 5th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID6.description: Extra 6th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID7.description: Extra 7th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID8.description: Extra 8th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DIA2_X.description: DIA_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_X.description: DIA_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_X.description: DIA_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DISBLMSK.description: This is a bitmask of driver types to disable. If a driver type is set in this mask then that driver will not try to find a sensor at startup

COMPASS_ENABLE.description: Setting this to Enabled(1) will enable the compass. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable the compass. Note that this is separate from COMPASS_USE. This will enable the low level senor, and will enable logging of magnetometer data. To use the compass for navigation you must also set COMPASS_USE to 1.

COMPASS_EXTERN2.display_name: Compass2 is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERN2.description: Configure second compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_EXTERN3.display_name: Compass3 is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERN3.description: Configure third compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_EXTERNAL.display_name: Compass is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERNAL.description: Configure compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. Set to 1 if the compass is externally connected. When externally connected the COMPASS_ORIENT option operates independently of the AHRS_ORIENTATION board orientation option. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_FLTR_RNG.display_name: Range in which sample is accepted
COMPASS_FLTR_RNG.description: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

COMPASS_LEARN.description: Enable or disable the automatic learning of compass offsets. You can enable learning either using a compass-only method that is suitable only for fixed wing aircraft or using the offsets learnt by the active EKF state estimator. If this option is enabled then the learnt offsets are saved when you disarm the vehicle. If InFlight learning is enabled then the compass with automatically start learning once a flight starts (must be armed). While InFlight learning is running you cannot use position control modes.

COMPASS_MOT2_X.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT2_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Y.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT2_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Z.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT2_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_X.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT3_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Y.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT3_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Z.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT3_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOTCT.description: Set motor interference compensation type to disabled, throttle or current.  Do not change manually.

COMPASS_MOT_X.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Y.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Z.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_ODI2_X.description: ODI_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_X.description: ODI_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_X.description: ODI_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_OFFS_MAX.description: This sets the maximum allowed compass offset in calibration and arming checks

COMPASS_OFS2_X.display_name: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS2_X.description: Offset to be added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Y.display_name: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS2_Y.description: Offset to be added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Z.display_name: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS2_Z.description: Offset to be added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_X.display_name: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS3_X.description: Offset to be added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Y.display_name: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS3_Y.description: Offset to be added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Z.display_name: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS3_Z.description: Offset to be added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_X.display_name: Compass offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS_X.description: Offset to be added to the compass x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Y.display_name: Compass offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS_Y.description: Offset to be added to the compass y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Z.display_name: Compass offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS_Z.description: Offset to be added to the compass z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the compass

bitmask.1: Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required)

COMPASS_ORIENT.description: The orientation of the first external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_ORIENT2.description: The orientation of a second external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_ORIENT3.description: The orientation of a third external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_PRIO1_ID.display_name: Compass device id with 1st order priority
COMPASS_PRIO1_ID.description: Compass device id with 1st order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_PRIO2_ID.display_name: Compass device id with 2nd order priority
COMPASS_PRIO2_ID.description: Compass device id with 2nd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_PRIO3_ID.display_name: Compass device id with 3rd order priority
COMPASS_PRIO3_ID.description: Compass device id with 3rd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

COMPASS_SCALE.description: Scaling factor for first compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_SCALE2.description: Scaling factor for 2nd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_SCALE3.description: Scaling factor for 3rd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

COMPASS_USE.description: Enable or disable the use of the compass (instead of the GPS) for determining heading

COMPASS_USE2.description: Enable or disable the secondary compass for determining heading.

COMPASS_USE3.description: Enable or disable the tertiary compass for determining heading.

COMPASS_PMOT1_X.description: Compensation for X axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT1_Y.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT1_Z.description: Compensation for Z axis of motor1

COMPASS_PMOT2_X.description: Compensation for X axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT2_Y.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT2_Z.description: Compensation for Z axis of motor2

COMPASS_PMOT3_X.description: Compensation for X axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT3_Y.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT3_Z.description: Compensation for Z axis of motor3

COMPASS_PMOT4_X.description: Compensation for X axis of motor4

COMPASS_PMOT4_Y.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor4

COMPASS_PMOT4_Z.description: Compensation for Z axis of motor4

COMPASS_PMOT_EXP.description: This is the exponential correction for the power output of the motor for per-motor compass correction

CUST_ROT1_PITCH.description: Custom euler pitch, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT1_ROLL.description: Custom euler roll, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT1_YAW.description: Custom euler yaw, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_PITCH.description: Custom euler pitch, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_ROLL.description: Custom euler roll, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

CUST_ROT2_YAW.description: Custom euler yaw, euler 321 (yaw, pitch, roll) ordering

DDS_MAX_RETRY.description: The maximum number of times the DDS client will attempt to ping the XRCE agent before exiting. Set to 0 to allow unlimited retries.

DDS_TIMEOUT_MS.description: The time in milliseconds the DDS client will wait for a response from the XRCE agent before reattempting.

DDS_USE_NS.description: When enabled, ROS 2 topic and service names include a v<MAV_SYSID> segment

DID_BARO_ACC.description: Barometer Vertical Accuracy when installed in the vehicle. Note this is dependent upon installation conditions and thus disabled by default

DID_CANDRIVER.description: DroneCAN driver index, 0 to disable DroneCAN

DID_MAVPORT.description: Serial port number to send OpenDroneID MAVLink messages to. Can be -1 if using DroneCAN.

bitmask.0: Vector Nav use uncompensated values for accel gyro and mag

EFI_COEF1.description: Used to calibrate fuel flow for MS protocol (Slope). This should be calculated from a log at constant fuel usage rate. Plot (ECYL[0].InjT*EFI.Rpm)/600.0 to get the duty_cycle. Measure actual fuel usage in cm^3/min, and set EFI_COEF1 = fuel_usage_cm3permin / duty_cycle

EFI_COEF2.description: Used to calibrate fuel flow for MS protocol (Offset). This can be used to correct for a non-zero offset in the fuel consumption calculation of EFI_COEF1

EFI_TYPE.description: What method of communication is used for EFI #1

EFI_THRLIN_COEF1.description: First Order Polynomial Coefficient. (=1, if throttle is first order polynomial trendline)

EFI_THRLIN_COEF2.description: Second Order Polynomial Coefficient (=0, if throttle is second order polynomial trendline)

EFI_THRLIN_COEF3.description: Third Order Polynomial Coefficient. (=0, if throttle is third order polynomial trendline)

EK2_ABIAS_P_NSE.description: This noise controls the growth of the vertical accelerometer delta velocity bias state error estimate. Increasing it makes accelerometer bias estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_ACC_P_NSE.description: This control disturbance noise controls the growth of estimated error due to accelerometer measurement errors excluding bias. Increasing it makes the flter trust the accelerometer measurements less and other measurements more.

EK2_ALT_M_NSE.description: This is the RMS value of noise in the altitude measurement. Increasing it reduces the weighting of the baro measurement and will make the filter respond more slowly to baro measurement errors, but will make it more sensitive to GPS and accelerometer errors.

EK2_ALT_SOURCE.description: Primary height sensor used by the EKF. If a sensor other than Baro is selected and becomes unavailable, then the Baro sensor will be used as a fallback. NOTE: The EK2_RNG_USE_HGT parameter can be used to switch to range-finder when close to the ground in conjunction with EK2_ALT_SOURCE = 0 or 2 (Baro or GPS).

EK2_BCN_DELAY.description: This is the number of msec that the range beacon measurements lag behind the inertial measurements. It is the time from the end of the optical flow averaging period and does not include the time delay due to the 100msec of averaging within the flow sensor.

EK2_BCN_I_GTE.description: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the range beacon measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_BCN_M_NSE.description: This is the RMS value of noise in the range beacon measurement. Increasing it reduces the weighting on this measurement.

EK2_CHECK_SCALE.description: This scales the thresholds that are used to check GPS accuracy before it is used by the EKF. A value of 100 is the default. Values greater than 100 increase and values less than 100 reduce the maximum GPS error the EKF will accept. A value of 200 will double the allowable GPS error.

EK2_EAS_I_GATE.description: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the airspeed measurement innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_EAS_M_NSE.description: This is the RMS value of noise in equivalent airspeed measurements used by planes. Increasing it reduces the weighting of airspeed measurements and will make wind speed estimates less noisy and slower to converge. Increasing also increases navigation errors when dead-reckoning without GPS measurements.

EK2_ENABLE.description: This enables EKF2. Enabling EKF2 only makes the maths run, it does not mean it will be used for flight control. To use it for flight control set AHRS_EKF_TYPE=2. A reboot or restart will need to be performed after changing the value of EK2_ENABLE for it to take effect.

EK2_FLOW_DELAY.description: This is the number of msec that the optical flow measurements lag behind the inertial measurements. It is the time from the end of the optical flow averaging period and does not include the time delay due to the 100msec of averaging within the flow sensor.

EK2_FLOW_I_GATE.description: This sets the percentage number of standard deviations applied to the optical flow innovation consistency check. Decreasing it makes it more likely that good measurements will be rejected. Increasing it makes it more likely that bad measurements will be accepted.

EK2_FLOW_M_NSE.description: This is the RMS value of noise and errors in optical flow measurements. Increasing it reduces the weighting on these measurements.

EK2_FLOW_USE.description: Controls if the optical flow data is fused into the 24-state navigation estimator OR the 1-state terrain height estimator.

EK2_GBIAS_P_NSE.description: This state  process noise controls growth of the gyro delta angle bias state error estimate. Increasing it makes rate gyro bias estimation faster and noisier.

EK2_GLITCH_RAD.description: This controls the maximum radial uncertainty in position between the value predicted by the filter and the value measured by the GPS before the filter position and velocity states are reset to the GPS. Making this value larger allows the filter to ignore larger GPS glitches but also means that non-GPS errors such as IMU and compass can create a larger error in position before the filter is forced back to the GPS position.

EK2_GPS_CHECK.description: This is a 1 byte bitmap controlling which GPS preflight checks are performed. Set to 0 to bypass all checks. Set to 255 perform all checks.
